name: Cancellation Funnel
length_rationale: A tight 2-page reactive funnel — a single branching survey page plus one personalized save page per branch. Intentionally minimal so it loads instantly inside the cancel/refund flow and resolves the objection in one or two clicks.

when_to_use.summary: Intercept a customer at the moment they try to cancel, refund, return, or downgrade. A quiz pinpoints the exact reason, then a tailored save page addresses that specific concern and offers a reason to stay — drastically reducing refund rate and churn.
goals: Reduce refund and chargeback rate on front-end and core offers
Deflect cancellations by resolving the specific objection that triggered them
Down-sell / pause instead of losing the customer entirely (save partial revenue)
Capture structured cancellation-reason data for product and offer improvement
signals: High refund or churn rate is the stated problem
Subscription / membership / continuity business losing members
Request mentions 'keep customers from leaving' or 'save the sale'
Need a branching 'why are you leaving?' survey with a tailored response
anti_signals: Acquiring new leads or buyers → use a front-end Lead/Tripwire/Webinar funnel
Nurturing/ascending an existing happy customer → use the Follow-Up Funnel
General feedback collection with no save intent → use Ask Campaign / Survey Funnel

page_structure.purpose: Catch the customer at the cancel/refund click and diagnose the single real reason they are leaving, so the next page can speak to exactly that objection. Each branch routes to a different save page.
key_elements: Empathetic, non-defensive headline ('Before you go — quick question so we can help')

Single-select reason quiz with the common churn reasons as branches: Too expensive / Didn't have time to use it / Didn't get results / Too complicated / Found another solution / Just exploring / Other
Conditional logic: each answer routes to its matching save page (Skill 44 quiz branching)
No multi-field friction — one tap to answer
Reason captured as structured data (tag/field) for reporting

page_structure.page: Personalized Save Page (one variant per reason branch)
page_structure.purpose: Address the exact concern the customer selected with empathy, a reframe, and a concrete reason to stay. This is the save-the-sale moment.
key_elements: Empathy open that names their specific concern (StoryBrand: acknowledge their problem, position brand as the guide)
Targeted reframe / false-belief break for that objection (Epiphany Bridge mini-story or proof)
A stay-incentive matched to the reason: bonus, onboarding help, extended trial, discount/downsell, pause option, or done-for-you setup
Short proof element (testimonial / result) relevant to that objection
Secondary/last-resort CTA: confirm cancel (with downsell offered first), so the flow still completes cleanly

branch_variants.save_play: Downsell to a lower tier, annual discount, or pause; reframe cost vs value with ROI proof
Offer pause/freeze, a quick-start path, or concierge onboarding so it isn't wasted
Onboarding call, success roadmap, or extended access guarantee; reframe expectations with a case study
Free setup / done-for-you help, simplified plan, or tutorial bundle
Differentiation reframe + matching offer or unique bonus they can't get elsewhere
Soft re-engagement: best-of content, extended trial, or route back into the Follow-Up Funnel

copy_framework.primary_persona: The Attractive Character as empathetic Guide — written in the brand's established voice but in service of the customer. Tone is understanding and helpful, never defensive or guilt-tripping.
copy_framework.primary_script: StoryBrand SB7 — frame the CUSTOMER as the hero who is about to fail at their goal, position the brand/product as the GUIDE with a plan that gets them the win, and make staying the clear call to action that avoids the failure of leaving.
secondary_scripts: Epiphany Bridge — a short story or proof per branch that breaks the specific false belief behind that objection
Copywriting Secrets (Jim Edwards) — objection-crushing and 'reason why' formulas, risk-reversal/guarantee language, and the downsell pitch
Expert Secrets false-belief pattern — identify the vehicle/internal/external false belief tied to each cancellation reason and rewrite it

per_page_assignment.script: StoryBrand acknowledgment open — name the problem, offer to help; minimal copy, maximum empathy
StoryBrand SB7 (Problem→Guide→Plan→CTA→Success/avoid-Failure) + Epiphany Bridge reframe + Copywriting Secrets objection-crush and downsell

copy_framework.notes: One objection, one page, one tailored offer. Lead with empathy, never argue. Stakes (StoryBrand failure) = the result they'll miss by leaving. Always give the stay-CTA more prominence than the cancel-CTA, but never trap the customer — the cancel path must remain available.

ghl_build_notes.skill6_role: Skill 6 instantiates a 2-step funnel: a survey/quiz step and a dynamic save step whose content swaps per branch (either separate save pages per reason, or one page with conditional sections).
skill6_pages: Page 2: Save page(s) — either N save pages (one per reason) linked by survey logic, or a single save page with conditional content blocks driven by the captured reason
skill44_widgets: Survey/Quiz widget (Skill 44) with single-select reason options and conditional routing to the matching save page
Reason answer mapped to a contact field/tag for segmentation and reporting
Calendar widget (Skill 44) embedded on 'no results' / 'too complicated' branches for an onboarding or save call
Order/downsell form widget (Skill 44) for the 'too expensive' branch (lower-tier or discounted plan), and a pause/freeze action where the billing platform supports it
automations: GHL trigger: cancel/refund/downgrade button or link enters the contact into the funnel and tags 'cancel-intent'
Workflow branch on survey answer → apply reason tag → show/route to matching save page
On 'stay' CTA: apply 'saved' tag, grant the promised bonus/discount/pause, remove cancel-intent, and (optionally) re-enroll into the Follow-Up Funnel nurture
On confirmed cancel: process cancellation, tag 'churned', and enroll in a win-back sequence
Reporting view: cancellation reasons + save-rate per branch for offer optimization
ghl_build_notes.notes: Keep both pages instant-loading and mobile-first — they appear mid-frustration. Surface the downsell/pause BEFORE the final cancel confirmation on every branch. Pipe all reason data to a report so the team can fix the upstream causes of churn.
